channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Micah Richards Honors Father Who Was His Greatest Fan Before World Cup Match
item.description: Micah Richards, a former England defender and BBC sports pundit, paid heartfelt tribute to his late father Lincoln, who passed away shortly before the BBC broadcast of England's World Cup semi-final d...

item.title: University Graduate Honors Boda Boda Rider for Years of Support
item.description: A university graduate named Moh surprised her boda boda rider Adnan by placing her graduation sash on him after three years of consistent support. The touching video, shared online, shows Moh running ...

item.title: Trump's Stock Purchases and Subsequent Promotions Raise Conflict of Interest Concerns
item.description: A CNN investigation reveals President Donald Trump's pattern of buying stocks in over 20 companies and later promoting them on his social media platforms, raising significant conflict of interest conc...

item.title: Kenya's Cash Handouts Spark Concerns Over 2027 Election Integrity
item.description: Kenya’s political landscape is increasingly dominated by multimillion-shilling cash handouts as the country prepares for the 2027 general election. Senior politicians, particularly those aligned with ...

item.title: Security Breach at NBC's 'Today' Show as Intruder Attacks Host
item.description: A stranger breached security at NBC's 'Today' Show on July 16, 2026, forcing his way into the Rockefeller Center headquarters and allegedly lunging at anchor Craig Melvin with a racial slur. The incid...

item.title: News Corp Australia blocks crawler bots with access restrictions
item.description: The article discusses an error message generated by News Corp Australia's website, which blocks crawler bots. The message instructs users to disable AdBlockers, ensure JavaScript is enabled, and use t...

item.title: eThekwini's Crime-and-Grime Initiative Achieves 28% Crime Reduction in Durban CBD
item.description: The eThekwini Municipality, in partnership with Ensure Ubuntu Cleaning Division, has achieved significant progress in reducing crime and improving cleanliness in Durban's Central Business District. A ...

item.title: Design trends and how to spot timeless home decor choices
item.description: The article explores the fleeting nature of interior design trends, using examples like battleship grey kitchens and white-on-white schemes that once dominated but now feel outdated. Experts like Sue ...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Results: No Winners in Major Jackpot Draws on July 16, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for either of the major lotto jackpot draws on July 16, 2026. The Superlotto 6/49 jackpot prize of P74,564,647.92 ...

item.title: Private Credit Growth Slows in Key Tanzanian Sectors
item.description: Private-sector credit growth in Tanzania decelerated in May as commercial banks adopted a more cautious lending approach, with financing momentum weakening across several critical economic sectors des...

item.title: NGOs Face Criticism for Hostile Behavior Toward Jews and Israel; Call to Label Anti-Israel Group as Terrorists

item.title: UK Experts Recommend Free MenB Vaccines for Teenagers to Combat Outbreak
item.description: UK health experts are recommending that all teenagers aged 15 receive a free meningitis B (MenB) vaccine, marking a shift from previous guidelines. This recommendation follows concerns over the UK's l...

item.title: Records Broken at the 2026 FIFA World Cup: Attendance, Goals, and Player Milestones
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has set numerous records, marking it as the largest tournament in its 96-year history with 48 teams. Attendance records were shattered, with 6.5 million fans watching matches a...

item.title: UN reports over 500 feared dead in Rohingya refugee boat incidents off Myanmar coast
item.description: The United Nations has reported that over 500 people are feared dead after two large shipwrecks off the coast of Myanmar since late June. The International Organization for Migration and UN High Commi...

item.title: KeNHA Demolishes Illegal Structures on Key Highway to Address Road Reserve Encroachment
item.description: The Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA) has carried out demolitions of illegal structures along the Kisii-Kadongo and Kadongo-Ahero A1 Road, targeting four trading centres where businesses had e...

item.title: A tribute to Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani: Qatar's visionary leader and legacy
item.description: This article is a heartfelt tribute to Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who played a pivotal role in shaping Qatar's modern identity. As a visionary leader, he recognized Qatar's potential long befo...

item.title: SC increases penalty for online psychological violence against women
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) recently ruled to increase the penalty for an individual convicted of psychological violence against his former partner, emphasizing that the offense was committed through socia...

item.title: NIS tackles visa violations and illegal stays by foreign nationals in Plateau
item.description: The Nigerian Immigration Service (NIS) in Plateau State has identified cases of foreign nationals overstaying their temporary work permits. Comptroller CIS Mary Abang revealed during a meeting with Go...

item.description: The article explores the intersection of political leadership and economic policies, highlighting how certain leaders leverage their positions to benefit from citizens' struggles. It examines cases wh...

item.title: Economic Challenges and Future of Canada's Roadside Attractions
item.description: Canada's roadside attractions, such as giant dinosaurs and oversized sausages, have long been a source of quirky tourism. These landmarks, many built between the 1950s and 1970s alongside the Trans-Ca...
